name: Basic White Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the milk, flour, cornstarch, and salt in a saucepan or screw-top jar.
2. Whisk or shake until there are no dry bits of cornstarch or flour remaining.
3. Stir-cook for about 2 minutes, until the mixture boils and thickens. Stir in the oil.
4. Season with white pepper if desired.
Each Serving (1/4 cup): 1 Milk Choice (2%)
Serve on cooked vegetables, meat, or fish. Use as a base for crepe fillings.
Variations:
Herb Sauce: Stir 1/2 teaspoon each of dried thyme, sweet basil, oregano, and parsley into the thickened Basic White Sauce.
Bechamel Sauce: Saute 1 tablespoon finely chopped onion in the 1 tablespoon oil when preparing Basic White Sauce, then stir into the thickened sauce.
Cream Salmon on Toast:
- Add 2 oz. flaked salmon, 2 tablespoons frozen peas, and 1 slice of toasted whole wheat bread to the white sauce.
- Mix well, then place over toast for a lunch option.
